home.title: share-env  secure .env file sharing
home.description: Store and share .env files with your team without pasting secrets in Slack. Encryption, workspace roles, and expiring links.

signup.title: Sign up
signup.description: Create a share.env account and start sharing .env files securely with your team.

hero.subtext: Secure secrets storage for your team: encryption, access roles, and expiring links instead of pasting API keys into chat.
hero.visualAlt: Abstract glass and metal vault representing encrypted .env file storage

howItWorks.title: From .env file to your team in four steps
howItWorks.lead: No more pasting API keys and database passwords into Slack. Upload once, and share.env handles encryption, links, and access for you.

steps.title: Upload your .env file
steps.description: Drag in your environment file or paste its contents. It's stored in your workspace, not in a chat thread.

steps.title: Encrypted at rest
steps.description: Every secret is encrypted in Supabase Storage and protected by row-level security, so only authorized requests ever reach it.

steps.title: Generate a share link
steps.description: Create a link with a high-entropy token, an expiry date, and revoke it instantly whenever access should end.

steps.title: Team access by role
steps.description: Invite teammates by email or workspace role  owner, editor, or viewer  and permissions are enforced on every request.

features.heading: Secret security, built for teams
features.subheading: Every .env file lives in a team workspace, not on one person's laptop  with access control and version history from day one.

items.title: File encryption
items.description: Your .env files are encrypted at rest, and Postgres Row Level Security enforces who can even read them  at the database layer, not just the UI.

items.title: Expiring links
items.description: Share a file without inviting someone to the team  every link carries an expiry date and can be revoked instantly, at any time.

items.title: Per-team access control
items.description: Owner, editor, and viewer roles decide who can edit secrets and who can only view them  enforced by database policies, not just hidden in the UI.

items.title: Version history
items.description: Every change to a .env file is recorded, so you always know who changed which secret and when  and can roll back to a previous version.

items.title: CLI & CI/CD integration
items.description: Pull the current environment variables straight into your deployment pipeline, without copying secrets between environments by hand.

testimonials.heading: Trusted by the devs and CTOs who own security
testimonials.subheading: Technical people who decide where their team's secrets live, on what convinced them to switch to share.env.

items.quote: API keys used to float around in Slack threads. Now every secret has an owner, a version history, and a link I can revoke in one click  security audits stopped being a nightmare.
items.name: Marta Wojcik

items.quote: The real difference is RLS at the database layer, not just the UI. Even if someone bypassed the frontend, they can't pull another team's environment variables  that's the bar I expect from a secrets tool.
items.name: Adam Nowicki

items.quote: Rollout took an afternoon: CLI wired into CI/CD, owner/editor/viewer roles for the team, and no more copying .env files between environments by hand. Simple without giving up control.
items.name: Julia Kaminska

signup.heading: Sign-up is coming soon
signup.body: We're building account creation and your first workspace. Check back shortly  or reach out if you'd like early access.

finalCta.heading: Stop pasting secrets into chat
finalCta.subtext: Create a workspace, upload your first .env file, and share it with your team in under two minutes.

faq.heading: Questions about security and data retention
faq.contactText: Send us a message  we usually reply to security and onboarding questions the same business day.

items.question: How exactly are .env files encrypted?
items.answer: Files live in Supabase Storage, where they're encrypted at rest. On top of that, Postgres Row Level Security ensures only requests authorized for your workspace can read the records  nobody else can access the contents, even with direct database access.

items.question: How long do you keep files and version history?
items.answer: Files and their version history are kept for as long as the workspace stays active. Deleting a workspace or a specific file also removes its stored versions and share links  we don't keep copies outside your account's active scope.

items.question: Can I see who had access to a given secret?
items.answer: Yes. Every change to a .env file is logged with who made it and when, and any share link can be revoked instantly, immediately blocking further access through that link.

items.question: Is self-hosting available?
items.answer: Right now share.env only runs as a hosted service  self-hosting isn't supported. The whole stack (database, auth, storage) runs on Supabase and Vercel, which lets us maintain a high security bar without you having to manage your own servers.

items.question: What happens to my secrets if I delete my account?
items.answer: Deleting your account permanently removes files you solely owned, along with their version history and active share links. Files belonging to workspaces with other members remain accessible to the remaining members with the right role.

footer.heading: Your team's secrets deserve better than Slack
footer.subtext: Upload your first .env file and see what secret sharing looks like with real access control.
footer.tagline: Built for teams who take secrets seriously.
footer.illustrationAlt: A glass and metal vault door left ajar, representing securely stored secrets
